content.full_text:
CITY OF
SIMI VALLEY

2929 Tapo Canyon Road, Simi Valley, California 93063 • (805) 583-6700

April 1, 1992


State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
The Travelers Business Center
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir or Madam:

On March 30, 1992, we requested an opinion by telephone regarding Form 721 of the
1991-92 Statement of Economic Interests and received a response from your
commission as follows:

The instructions for "Schedule D-1 Income-Travel Payments, Advances,
Reimbursements" state that "Travel payments received from a state, local, or
federal government agency, for which you have provided equal or greater
consideration, are not required to be disclosed. If you have not provided equal
or greater consideration for the payments, you may be required to disclose the
payments as gifts."

The instructions for "Schedule D-Income" state that "you are not required to
disclose the following types of income: salary or reimbursements for expenses and
per diem from a federal, state or local government agency."

The question we asked was whether a Planning Commissioner needs to include travel
payments from the City as income on Form 721 for a League of California Cities
Planning Commissioner Institute which that Commissioner attended.

The response received was that the Planning Commissioner must include these
travel payments from the City as income on Form 721 unless that Commissioner has
paid an amount equal to fifty percent or greater of the cost of that trip. We
were told that this rule had been changed in 1991.

At this time we are requesting this opinion in writing. It is a common practice
for cities, counties, and other governmental agencies to send both elected and
appointed officials to conferences, meetings, and training sessions which involve
travel, accommodations and meals. Since it is the public entity which employs

GREGORY STRATTON, Mayor • BILL DAVIS, Mayor Pro Tem • SANDI WEBB, Council Member • JUDY MIKELS, Council Member • MICHAEL W. PIPER, Council Member


Fair Political Practices Commission
April 1, 1992
Page 2


the official which provides the travel, it is difficult to understand what
Political Reform Act purpose is served by requiring disclosure of the public
entity's cost of such a trip as income to the official. We therefore request
your written response because of the important implications of your telephone
response.

Sincerely,

Marjorie Baxter
Assistant City Attorney II

MB/nd


ltr2268
